title: GraphOTTER: Evolving LLM-based Graph Reasoning for Complex Table Question Answering

task_A_numeric.topic: What does this paper claim about GraphOTTER's performance gain over the best baseline on HiTab and AIT-QA?
task_A_numeric.exact_quote: GraphOTTER achieves a notable improvement in complex table question answering, exhibiting an average performance gain of +4.77% over the best baseline.

task_A_prose.topic: What does this paper claim about how combining graph representations with explicit reasoning improves table question answering?
task_A_prose.exact_quote: combining graph representations with explicit reasoning creates a powerful synergy that harnesses the flexibility of the graph while mitigating its inherent complexity

task_B.topic: GraphOTTER's performance improvement over the best baseline
task_B.true_claim: GraphOTTER outperforms the best baseline by +9.07% on HiTab and only +1.63% on AIT-QA, for an average of +4.77%.

task_C.figure: Table 2 (main results on HiTab and AIT-QA)
task_C.note: repo exists with eval scripts, command not in paper prose
